commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can address the no-assistant recovery bug; the backup-retention policy still needs maintainer acceptance before merge.; Needs attention: The patch adds a persistent transcript backup sidecar that keeps sensitive session bytes, so the retention behavior needs explicit maintainer acceptance before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Rewrite recovered no-assistant files before the initializer... (sha=20ae0d331ab3ee528b4e9a280f808473f33b1479)
commands.review_summary:
The PR recovers malformed first-line session JSONL headers into a valid transcript with a `.corrupt-*` backup, guards embedded-runner reset behavior, changes the first full-buffer persist from append to write, and adds session-manager regression tests.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +151. Total +241 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue provides a concrete corrupt-header JSONL shape, and source inspection shows current main truncates it while the current PR still aborts the no-assistant recovered shape in the production open-then-initialize path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can address the no-assistant recovery bug; the backup-retention policy still needs maintainer acceptance before merge.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve corrupt-header transcripts
target.body:
## Summary

- Prevent `prepareSessionManagerForRun` from clearing an existing session transcript when the on-disk header is unreadable.
- Surface the corrupt-header condition instead, preserving the original transcript bytes for recovery/inspection.
- Add a regression test that writes a malformed first JSONL line plus a persisted user message and proves the file is unchanged after the guarded path rejects.

## Linked context

Closes #89037.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: resuming a session whose first JSONL header line is corrupted could reach the no-assistant reset path and truncate the transcript.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at current `origin/main` with the real filesystem-backed `prepareSessionManagerForRun` helper and a temp JSONL transcript file.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`.
- Evidence after fix: the new regression passes and asserts the original malformed transcript string remains byte-for-byte unchanged after the helper rejects.
- Observed result after fix: the helper throws `Refusing to reset session transcript with unreadable header` before `fs.writeFile(..., "")` can run.
- What was not tested: a live production resume against a real user transcript was not performed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I avoided using private/local user session transcripts; the regression uses a disposable temp transcript with the same destructive file operation path.
- Before evidence: the existing no-assistant branch unconditionally called `fs.writeFile(params.sessionFile, "", "utf-8")` for any `hadSessionFile && header && !hasAssistant` state.

## Tests and validation

- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed 1 Vitest shard / 3 tests.
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-manager-init.test.ts` passed.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, corrupt session-header resumes now fail before destructive reset instead of silently wiping the file.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: preserving the existing first-run session-manager workaround. The guard only reads and validates the existing first nonblank line before the reset; valid pre-created session headers still follow the old reset path.

## Current review state

Ready for review. No bot or reviewer comments have been addressed yet.
target.author: charles-openclaw
